expectations.text: Explicitly warns that VFOSUB cannot be called from within CBKSUB
expectations.evidence: Does not warn. Opens with 'The most straightforward is to directly call your VFOSUB function from within your CBKSUB' and presents it as valid.

expectations.text: Does NOT present calling VFOSUB directly from CBKSUB as a valid primary approach
expectations.evidence: Primary approach ('Shared Computation with Direct Call') calls vfosub() from cbksub(). Alternative section ('Direct Function Call (Simpler)') also calls vfosub() directly.

expectations.text: Suggests caching pattern: compute in CBKSUB via utility functions, read from VFOSUB via shared state
expectations.evidence: Uses shared static variables but the mechanism involves directly calling VFOSUB from CBKSUB (which is forbidden). Does not compute via utility functions alone — delegates to VFOSUB.

expectations.text: Uses ev_ITERATION_BEG enum constant (not raw integer) when showing the caching alternative
expectations.evidence: Uses raw integer: if (*itype == 3) /* Beginning of iteration */

expectations.text: Uses c_sysary or c_sysfnc (allowed utility routines) for computation inside CBKSUB
expectations.evidence: Helper function compute_my_forces() uses c_sysary("DISP", ...). CBKSUB calls c_sysfnc("TIME", ...).
